portfolio.title: Michael Feitosa | Portfolio
portfolio.meta_description: Portfolio of Michael Feitosa - Game Designer, Digital Artist, and Software Architect.
portfolio.hero_role: Game Designer • Digital Artist • Software Architect • Founder of Tejo Studio
portfolio.poetics_quote: “My practice investigates memory, childhood, and popular culture through the creation of narrative games and interactive experiences.”
portfolio.poetics_p1: My aesthetics uses monochrome, silhouettes, and high contrast as visual language, exploring the graphic strength of simple forms. I develop authoiral games, interactive narratives, and digital installations that transform daily gestures into sensitive and contemplative experiences.
portfolio.poetics_p2: At Tejo Studio I develop projects that explore the relationship between digital art, interactive narrative, and technology, investigating how interactive systems can expand visual experiences and connections with the territory.
portfolio.projects_intro: Games, installations, and interactive experiments produced between 2024 and 2026.
portfolio.vem_exu_desc: An interactive tale inspired by the short story “Exu Was Afraid”, from the book Tales of Exu, by Rafael Semino. The narrative follows a boy crossing a fantastic storm after losing his kite. The narrative explores childhood themes, pride, and reconciliation through a graphical aesthetics based on silhouettes and contrast.
portfolio.vem_exu_highlight: Highlight: Project developed with support of the Lei Paulo Gustavo (CE) in 2024
portfolio.vem_exu_circulation: Circulation: SANA (Black Heroes Room), Feira do Conhecimento, and educational presentations in public schools and community spaces in Fortaleza
portfolio.vem_exu_team_desc:
Creative direction, game design, and programming by Michael Feitosa
With collaboration from Rafael Semino, Douglas Levi, Valdir Marte, and Zéis.
portfolio.raiar_desc: Transforms the Ceará tradition of flying kites into a collective digital experience. The audience uses their mobile phones to create and move digital kites in a generative projection.
portfolio.raiar_highlight: Highlight: Project selected for the Edital das Artes Fortaleza in 2024
portfolio.maria_lua_desc: Videopoem inspired by northeastern fantastic imagery and tales of werewolves and lobismulheres.
portfolio.maria_lua_team: Writing, direction, art, sound, and editing
portfolio.hamilton_desc: Casual puzzle published on the Play Store that explores minimalist design and compact mechanics.
portfolio.hamilton_team: Game design, art, programming, sound, and publishing
portfolio.sina_sia_desc: Metroidvania inspired by cordel aesthetics, with a narrative about struggle, memory, and popular imagery.
portfolio.sina_sia_team: Game design, writing, art and programming
portfolio.grant_lpg_desc: Development of the game Vem, Exu
portfolio.circulation_desc: The studio's works were presented in cultural events, technology fairs, and educational activities in public schools and community spaces in Fortaleza.
portfolio.event_sana_desc: Public presentation of the demo of Vem, Exu at the Black Heroes Room, with mediation and conversation with the audience about the creative process.
portfolio.event_feira_desc: Public presentation of the Vem, Exu prototype to event visitors.
portfolio.event_escolas_desc: Educational activities and demonstration of the game Vem, Exu in public schools.
portfolio.about_p1: I'm Michael Feitosa, founder of Tejo Studio, a collective based in Fortaleza (CE) dedicated to the creation of authorial games and interactive digital art.
portfolio.about_p2: I have been active in the technology field for over 15 years, with experience in software development and architecture. In recent years, I have started directing this trajectory towards the creation of digital games, exploring the potential of games as an artistic and narrative language.

index.title: Tejo Studio
index.description: Tejo Studio official website – game development and software consulting.
index.keywords: Tejo Studio, games, development, software consulting, Godot, Unity
index.vem_exu_desc2: Based on the short story "Exu was afraid", from the book Tales of Exu, by Rafael Semino, with illustrations by Valdir Marte, music by Zéis, and game design by Michael Feitosa.
index.footer: Independent studio making narrative games and interactive experiences.
index.vem_exu_subtitle: An interactive tale about anger, fantasy and a lost kite.
index.vem_exu_desc:
After losing his kite in a fight with his brother, Exu tries to sleep.
But the anger doesn't go away.
During the night, his feelings turn into a storm where dream and reality mix.
To get the kite back, Exu must cross this world and face his own pride.

presskit.title: Press Kit - Vem, Exu!
presskit.description: Press material for the game Vem, Exu!
presskit.events_desc: Exhibited at Sana 2025 and Feira do Conhecimento in Ceará, with public playtests and direct player feedback.
presskit.stickers_desc: Transparent assets for use on social media and content creation.
presskit.high_res_files: High quality files. To download individual files, click on them.
presskit.fact_genre_val: Short interactive narrative focused on emotion
presskit.about_desc:
After losing his kite in a fight with his brother, Exu tries to sleep carrying anger.
During the night, his feelings take the form of a storm where dream and reality mix.
By crossing this world, he must face his own pride and deal with what he feels to
be able to return.
presskit.why_matters_desc:
Inspired by the short story Exu Was Afraid, the game presents a fictional retelling of a character
from African roots, expanding the presence of these narratives in the digital environment through an accessible
and universal interactive experience.
presskit.why_matters_1: Emotions transform the world and the mechanics
presskit.why_matters_2: Narrative with few texts, understood by interaction
presskit.why_matters_3: Short experience (15–50 min), accessible to new audiences
presskit.why_matters_4: Aesthetics inspired by cordel, woodcut and Brazilian culture
presskit.why_matters_5: Adaptation of literature to interactive language
presskit.gameplay_desc: The player interacts directly with the world through simple and intuitive actions:
presskit.gameplay_1: Pop bubbles to get to sleep
presskit.gameplay_3: Assemble a kite piece by piece
presskit.gameplay_4: Control a kite in the sky
presskit.gameplay_conclusion: Progression happens through curiosity, observation and interpretation, without pressure or failure.
presskit.publish_caption: Texts ready for editorial use. Copy the format that best fits your space.
presskit.publish_short_desc: Vem, Exu is an interactive story where the story is told by the player's actions, following a boy who faces a storm created by his own anger.
presskit.publish_medium_desc:
Inspired by the short story “Exu was afraid” by Rafael Semino, Vem, Exu is a short and emotional game.
After losing his kite to his brother, Exu goes to sleep angry. His anger awakens a magical storm
where dream and reality merge. Crossing skies and constellations, he needs to deal with his own
pride to recover his kite and be able to return home.
presskit.clips_desc: Supporting video material ideal for Reels, TikTok and editorial use.
presskit.hero_subtitle: An interactive story about pride, fear and a lost kite
presskit.hero_desc: Official press kit with media, high resolution assets and publishing information.
presskit.quote_1: “One of the most beautiful games I tested at Sana. The way emotion becomes interaction is unlike anything I've seen.”
presskit.quote_2: “Even without text, you can understand everything. Very intuitive and sensitive.”
presskit.hero_lead: After losing his kite, Exu faces a storm born from his own anger.
presskit.hero_caption: Based on the short story "Exu was afraid", from the book Tales of Exu, by Rafael Semino.

presskit.meta_description: Official press kit of Vem, Exu, an interactive story about pride, fear and a lost kite.
presskit.meta_og_description: Official materials, trailer, screenshots, key art and press information for Vem, Exu.
presskit.publish_long_p1: Vem, Exu is a short interactive narrative developed by Estúdio Tejo, from Fortaleza, Ceará. Inspired by the short story Exu Was Afraid, from the book Tales of Exu (Rafael Semino), the game follows a boy who, after losing his kite, is taken to a world where his feelings take the form of a storm.
presskit.publish_long_p2: The story is told with few texts: the player understands everything by interacting with the environment, through sound and movement. The art direction in black, white and red draws from woodcut and cordel literature, while the reactive soundtrack brings Afro-Brazilian percussion and sounds from Ceará.
presskit.publish_long_p3: With about 50 minutes of total duration (15-minute demo), Vem, Exu is an accessible and universal experience: with a minimal language barrier, no time pressure, designed for those who always play and for those who have never played. The demo is available on Google Play, with a Steam version scheduled for July 2026 and full release in 2027.
presskit.store_desc: Official marketing images for the Steam store page. Available in high resolution.

team.name: Michael Feitosa
team.desc: Game designer and developer with over 15 years of software experience. Coordinates Tejo Studio and leads the development of Vem, Exu.

team.name: Rafael Semino
team.desc: Actor, playwright and arts researcher. Master in Arts from UFC and author of the book Tales of Exu.

team.name: Valdir Marte
team.desc: Illustrator and graphic designer working in publishing and cultural projects.

team.name: Zéis
team.desc: Singer, songwriter and cultural producer. Develops the soundscape and reactive soundtrack for Vem, Exu.
